details: Package name 'clx-cookieparser' and the declared repository field 'expressjs/cookieparser' appear to impersonate the widely-used express/cookie-parser package. The shipped index.js is a verbatim copy of upstream cookie-parser source (retaining TJ Holowaychuk attribution). No installer-harm payload is present: there are no lifecycle scripts (preinstall/install/postinstall), no network I/O, no obfuscation, no credential reads, and no environment-variable scraping. The package functions as a thin re-publish of the legitimate middleware under a confusingly similar name and a misleading repository reference. Name-confusion alone, without an attached harmful payload, is a subjective signal best resolved by a human reviewer assessing intent and registry policy.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
